words.definition: Relating to the method and practice of teaching; pertaining to educational theory and instruction.
words.context: The simple narrative taught in every history class is demonstrably false and pedagogically classist.

words.definition: Relating to solipsism; the philosophical idea that only one's own mind is sure to exist, or the tendency to view everything in relation to oneself.
words.context: Why do you rich fucking white people insist on seeing every socio-political conflict through the myopic lens of your own self-actualization?

words.definition: Near-sighted; lacking foresight or intellectual insight; narrowly focused.
words.context: Why do you rich fucking white people insist on seeing every socio-political conflict through the myopic lens of your own self-actualization?

words.definition: A psychological state in which the external world feels unreal, dreamlike, or distorted; often associated with dissociative disorders.
words.context: Total disassociation, fully out your mind, Googling 'derealization,' hating what you find.

words.definition: A mental process of disconnecting from one's thoughts, feelings, memories, or sense of identity; a defense mechanism.
words.context: Total disassociation, fully out your mind.

words.definition: Relating to agoraphobia; fear or anxiety about places or situations that might cause panic, helplessness, or embarrassment.
words.context: Full agoraphobic, losing focus, cover blown.

words.definition: To attribute human characteristics, emotions, or intentions to non-human entities, animals, or objects.
words.context: Why is Pluto just a fucking dog? Why–did they just forget to anthropomorphize him?

words.definition: An act of speaking one's thoughts aloud when by oneself or regardless of any hearers, especially in a dramatic context.
words.context: He could stack metaphor five, six at a time and rhyme into the very last line of a soliloquy.

words.definition: The point at which something is complete or finalized; archaic/literary usage referring to completion or fulfillment.
words.context: 'Tis a consummation devoutly to be wish'd. To die, to sleep.

words.definition: The provision of evidence or proof to support a claim; in theology, the conversion of bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ.
words.context: Transgendered substantiation.

words.definition: One who speaks irreverently about God or sacred things; one who shows contempt or lack of reverence for religious beliefs.
words.context: I'm a heretic, and I'm on fire. I'm a blasphemer post-Katrina.

words.definition: A measure of disorder or randomness in a system; in thermodynamics, the degree to which energy is unavailable for work.
words.context: I've got an eating disorder. Yeah, more like nutritional entropy.

words.definition: Relating to quantum mechanics; the smallest discrete unit of any physical property; involving probabilistic rather than deterministic behavior.
words.context: My penis is so small I often have trouble finding it because it's so gratefully influenced by quantum mechanical fluctuations in the fabric of space-time.

words.definition: Impossible to satisfy or quench; always wanting more; from Latin 'in-' (not) + 'satiare' (to satisfy).
words.context: Mommy let you use her iPad, you were barely two, and it did all the things we designed it to do. Now look at you, insatiable you.

words.definition: Relating to a political and economic philosophy emphasizing free-market capitalism, deregulation, and reduction in government spending.
words.context: And neoliberal fascists are destroying the left.

words.definition: One who derives sexual gratification from observing others; more broadly, someone who enjoys observing the private lives of others.
words.context: I need you like a voyeur needs a branch, like boys tossing salad needs a little bit of Neverland Ranch.

words.definition: A primate of the family Hominidae, which includes humans and their extinct ancestors and relatives.
words.context: You're making me devolve to a homo erectus. Yeah, hominid tree.

words.definition: Relating to the aristocracy; a class of people holding exceptional rank and privileges, especially the hereditary nobility.
words.context: If we met in 1780, I was a white southern aristocratic plantation owner.

words.definition: Relating to the combination and interaction of social and political factors; involving both societal and governmental dimensions.
words.context: Have you not been fucking listening? We are entrenched in– [socio-political conflict].

words.definition: One who holds beliefs contrary to established religious doctrine; a dissenter from orthodox teachings.
words.context: I'm a heretic, and I'm on fire.
